speaker: MACBETH
text: If it were done when ’tis done, then ’twere well

speaker: MACBETH
text: It were done quickly. If th’ assassination

speaker: MACBETH
text: Could trammel up the consequence and catch

speaker: MACBETH
text: With his surcease success, that but this blow

speaker: MACBETH
text: Might be the be-all and the end-all here,

speaker: MACBETH
text: But here, upon this bank and shoal of time,

speaker: MACBETH
text: We’d jump the life to come. But in these cases

speaker: MACBETH
text: We still have judgment here, that we but teach

speaker: MACBETH
text: Bloody instructions, which, being taught, return

speaker: MACBETH
text: To plague th’ inventor. This even-handed justice

speaker: MACBETH
text: Commends th’ ingredience of our poisoned chalice

speaker: MACBETH
text: To our own lips. He’s here in double trust:

speaker: MACBETH
text: First, as I am his kinsman and his subject,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Strong both against the deed; then, as his host,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Who should against his murderer shut the door,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Not bear the knife myself. Besides, this Duncan

speaker: MACBETH
text: Hath borne his faculties so meek, hath been

speaker: MACBETH
text: So clear in his great office, that his virtues

speaker: MACBETH
text: Will plead like angels, trumpet-tongued, against

speaker: MACBETH
text: The deep damnation of his taking-off;

speaker: MACBETH
text: And pity, like a naked newborn babe

speaker: MACBETH
text: Striding the blast, or heaven’s cherubin horsed

speaker: MACBETH
text: Upon the sightless couriers of the air,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shall blow the horrid deed in every eye,

speaker: MACBETH
text: That tears shall drown the wind. I have no spur

speaker: MACBETH
text: To prick the sides of my intent, but only

speaker: MACBETH
text: Vaulting ambition, which o’erleaps itself

speaker: MACBETH
text: And falls on th’ other—

speaker: MACBETH
text: How now, what news?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: He has almost supped. Why have you left the chamber?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Hath he asked for me?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Know you not he has?

speaker: MACBETH
text: We will proceed no further in this business.

speaker: MACBETH
text: He hath honored me of late, and I have bought

speaker: MACBETH
text: Golden opinions from all sorts of people,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which would be worn now in their newest gloss,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Not cast aside so soon.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Was the hope drunk

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Wherein you dressed yourself? Hath it slept since?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And wakes it now, to look so green and pale

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: At what it did so freely? From this time

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Such I account thy love. Art thou afeard

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: To be the same in thine own act and valor

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: As thou art in desire? Wouldst thou have that

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Which thou esteem’st the ornament of life

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And live a coward in thine own esteem,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Letting “I dare not” wait upon “I would,”

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Like the poor cat i’ th’ adage?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Prithee, peace.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I dare do all that may become a man.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Who dares do more is none.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What beast was ’t, then,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That made you break this enterprise to me?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: When you durst do it, then you were a man;

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And to be more than what you were, you would

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Be so much more the man. Nor time nor place

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Did then adhere, and yet you would make both.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: They have made themselves, and that their fitness now

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Does unmake you. I have given suck, and know

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: How tender ’tis to love the babe that milks me.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: I would, while it was smiling in my face,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Have plucked my nipple from his boneless gums

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And dashed the brains out, had I so sworn as you

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Have done to this.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If we should fail—

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: We fail?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: But screw your courage to the sticking place

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And we’ll not fail. When Duncan is asleep

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: (Whereto the rather shall his day’s hard journey

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Soundly invite him), his two chamberlains

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Will I with wine and wassail so convince

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That memory, the warder of the brain,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Shall be a fume, and the receipt of reason

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: A limbeck only. When in swinish sleep

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Their drenchèd natures lies as in a death,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What cannot you and I perform upon

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Th’ unguarded Duncan? What not put upon

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: His spongy officers, who shall bear the guilt

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Of our great quell?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Bring forth men-children only,

speaker: MACBETH
text: For thy undaunted mettle should compose

speaker: MACBETH
text: Nothing but males. Will it not be received,

speaker: MACBETH
text: When we have marked with blood those sleepy two

speaker: MACBETH
text: Of his own chamber and used their very daggers,

speaker: MACBETH
text: That they have done ’t?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Who dares receive it other,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: As we shall make our griefs and clamor roar

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Upon his death?

speaker: MACBETH
text: I am settled and bend up

speaker: MACBETH
text: Each corporal agent to this terrible feat.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Away, and mock the time with fairest show.

speaker: MACBETH
text: False face must hide what the false heart doth know.
